The proposal targets a multidisciplinary review panel, including experts in education policy and ethics, not just AI specialists. detailed_results.explanation: A two-way ANOVA is the most appropriate technique as it allows for the simultaneous assessment of the main effects of two independent categorical variables (UI change and user segment) on a continuous outcome (conversion rate), as well as their interaction effect. This captures a more nuanced understanding than simpler tests.
detailed_results.question: A company launched a new feature on its e-commerce website, which involved a significant change to the application-specific interface of the product page. They want to determine if this UI change significantly impacted conversion rates, considering different user segments (e.g., desktop vs. mobile users). They have collected conversion data from both segments before and after the change. Which statistical analysis technique is MOST appropriate to identify the effect of the UI change while accounting for user segments and their potential interaction?
